title: mentha pulegium subsp. vulgaris
intro: Mentha pulegium subsp. vulgaris, also known as the wild or common mint, is a subspecies of the water mint plant (Mentha pulegium) native to Europe and other parts of the world. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of menthone and pulegone compounds, which have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of wild mint include relief from digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. Wild mint can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and traditional medicine.
